strategy_research: {"strategies": [{"name": "Vigilant Asset Allocation (VAA-G4)", "description": "Breadth momentum strategy that uses a weighted composite momentum score (1/3/6/12-month, heavily weighting recent month) across a 4-asset offensive universe. Rotates entirely to defensive bonds when any offensive asset shows negative momentum \u2014 a 'breadth' crash filter rather than individual trend filter.", "evidence": ["Keller & Keuning (2017) 'Breadth Momentum and Vigilant Asset Allocation' SSRN:3002624 \u2014 backtested 1925\u20132017", "Keller & Keuning (2018) DAA extension 'Canary Universe' SSRN:3212862 \u2014 improved CAGR with lower cash drag", "Allocate Smartly independent backtest: Sharpe ~1.10, max drawdown <15%"], "historical_sharpe": "~1.10 (VAA-G4, independent backtest); DAA variant improves CAGR with ~25-30% avg cash vs 50-60% for VAA", "market_conditions": "Works: sustained bear markets, trending momentum regimes (2000-02, 2008-09). Fails: rapid V-shaped recoveries (March 2020), whipsaw markets, simultaneous bond-equity declines (2022 where TLT fell alongside SPY)", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "AGG", "GLD", "TLT"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Cross-Asset Time-Series Momentum with Volatility Targeting", "description": "Three-layer strategy: (1) rank all ETFs by risk-adjusted composite momentum (1/3/6/12-month), (2) apply absolute trend filter \u2014 only hold assets above 200-day SMA, else hold cash, (3) size each position inversely to its 21-day realized volatility so each contributes equal risk, scaled to 10% target portfolio vol.", "evidence": ["Pitk\u00e4j\u00e4rvi, Suominen & Vaittinen (2020) 'Cross-Asset Signals and Time Series Momentum' Journal of Financial Economics 136(1):63-85 \u2014 cross-asset TSMOM yields ~45% higher Sharpe than standard TSMOM", "Harvey, Rattray, Sinclair & Van Hemert (2018) 'The Impact of Volatility Targeting' Journal of Portfolio Management \u2014 vol targeting materially improves Sharpe for equity/credit strategies", "Moskowitz, Ooi & Pedersen (AQR, 2012) 'Time Series Momentum' JFE \u2014 foundational evidence across 58 futures, Sharpe ~0.95", "Hurst, Ooi & Pedersen (AQR, 2017) 'A Century of Evidence on Trend-Following' JPM \u2014 positive in 8/10 major crisis periods"], "historical_sharpe": "~1.0\u20131.2 for cross-asset variant (+45% vs basic TSMOM); standard TSMOM ~0.70\u20130.95 on futures", "market_conditions": "Works: trending macro regimes, sustained directional moves in any asset class (2001-08, 2014-18, 2020-21). Fails: range-bound choppy markets, momentum crashes after sharp reversals, simultaneous multi-asset declines (2022)", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "GLD", "TLT", "EFA", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Risk Parity with Volatility Targeting (SPY-TLT-GLD Triad)", "description": "Weights assets inversely proportional to their 21-day realized volatility so each contributes equal risk. The SPY/TLT/GLD triad achieves strong diversification because bonds historically offset equity drawdowns. Total portfolio scaled to 10% target annualized volatility using cash as the balancing lever.", "evidence": ["Maillard, Roncalli & Teiletche (2010) 'On the Properties of Equally-Weighted Risk Contributions Portfolios' Journal of Portfolio Management \u2014 foundational ERC paper", "Logical Invest SPY-TLT Universal Investment Strategy backtest: Sharpe ~1.28", "S&P SPDJI 'Indexing Risk Parity Strategies' \u2014 higher Sharpe vs 60/40 across multiple periods", "Harvey et al. (2018) \u2014 vol targeting improves Sharpe particularly for equity and credit"], "historical_sharpe": "~1.28 (SPY-TLT vol-targeted, Logical Invest backtest); ~1.26 for Equal Risk Contribution vs ~0.50 for SPY alone", "market_conditions": "Works: negative or zero stock-bond correlation (2000-2019 ideal), flight-to-quality regimes, inflationary or deflationary trending environments. Fails critically: 2022 \u2014 TLT down 33% simultaneously with SPY down 18%, the strategy's worst modern failure; any environment with positive stock-bond correlation", "instruments": ["SPY", "TLT", "GLD"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Regime-Based Rotation: VIX + Yield Curve", "description": "Uses two orthogonal macro signals to switch between risk-on and risk-off allocations. Signal 1: VIX level and term structure (contango = complacency, backwardation = panic/capitulation). Signal 2: 10Y-2Y yield curve slope (steepening = expand into equities, flat/inverted = rotate to TLT/GLD). The combined VIX-yield-curve polar coordinate angle outperforms either signal alone for recession prediction 6-18 months ahead.", "evidence": ["Cieslak, Morse & Vissing-J\u00f8rgensen (2019) \u2014 equity premium earned entirely in weeks 0/2/4/6 of FOMC cycle; regime-sensitive timing adds value", "International Journal of Forecasting (2023) 'Predicting Recessions Using VIX-Yield Curve Cycles' \u2014 out-of-sample from 1950-2022, outperforms standalone yield curve", "MDPI (2026) Canadian equity sector rotation study \u2014 median Sharpe 0.922 vs 0.624 for equal-weighted buy-and-hold", "ML regime classification (2024 arXiv) \u2014 random forest achieves ~72.7% accuracy in volatility regime identification"], "historical_sharpe": "~0.92 (sector rotation with regime signals vs ~0.62 for buy-and-hold); combined VIX+curve signal significantly outperforms either alone", "market_conditions": "Works: pronounced macro cycles with clear leading indicators (2000-02, 2008-09, COVID 2020). Fails: abrupt externally-driven shocks, yield curve false positives (2022-23 inversion without immediate recession), contradictory VIX/curve signals", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "TLT", "GLD", "AGG"], "timeframe": "weekly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Stacked Signal Ensemble (Momentum + Trend + Vol Regime)", "description": "Combines multiple individually weak signals whose errors are independent, creating a higher-Sharpe ensemble. Signal stack: 12-month momentum (252d), 3-month momentum (63d), 1-month reversal (21d), 50/200-day SMA crossover, volatility ratio (21d/252d), yield curve slope, and VIX level. Uses 'Mixed' combination (separate signal portfolios blended with fixed weights) rather than 'Integrated' (signals merged before portfolio construction) \u2014 GSAM shows Mixed outperforms Integrated by ~0.08 Sharpe for momentum-carry combinations.", "evidence": ["Goldman Sachs Asset Management (2018) 'How to Combine Investment Signals in Long/Short Strategies' \u2014 Mixed method outperforms Integrated by ~0.08 Sharpe", "Asness, Moskowitz & Pedersen (AQR, 2013) 'Value and Momentum Everywhere' Journal of Finance \u2014 diversified signal combination across asset classes", "Hoffstein & Gordillo (2021) 'Return Stacking' ReSolve/Newfound \u2014 28% relative vol reduction from uncorrelated signal overlay", "arXiv (January 2025) 'Follow the Leader: Network Momentum' \u2014 OOS Sharpe ~0.645 net vs MACD benchmark 2005-2024"], "historical_sharpe": "0.64\u20131.0+ depending on signal combination and period; single momentum signal ~0.60-0.80; ensemble adds ~0.08-0.20 Sharpe via diversification", "market_conditions": "Works: diversified market regimes \u2014 each signal contributes alpha in its own best conditions and the ensemble smooths across them. Fails: all signals correlate during fast crashes (March 2020); parameter overfitting risk is high; factor crowding post-publication reduces alpha", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "VTI", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG", "EFA"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "high"}, {"name": "Hierarchical Risk Parity (HRP \u2014 L\u00f3pez de Prado)", "description": "Machine learning portfolio construction using hierarchical clustering on the correlation matrix to group similar assets, then recursive bisection to allocate risk down the dendrogram. Avoids matrix inversion (Markowitz's key failure point), works on ill-conditioned covariance matrices, and respects correlation structure unlike naive risk parity. Applied to the full 8-ETF universe with 60-day rolling covariance.", "evidence": ["L\u00f3pez de Prado (2016) 'Building Diversified Portfolios that Outperform Out-of-Sample' Journal of Portfolio Management, SSRN:2708678 \u2014 Monte Carlo shows lower OOS variance than CLA even though minimum-variance is CLA's stated objective", "Noguer i Alonso (2025) 'Return-Adjusted HRP' SSRN:5370624 \u2014 RA-HRP achieves Sharpe 1.336 on 15 US stocks 2010-2024", "Lohre et al. \u2014 standard HRP ~0.94 net vs ~1.26 net for ERC; high turnover (~18% vs 5%) erodes net Sharpe"], "historical_sharpe": "~0.94 net (standard HRP); Return-Adjusted HRP (RA-HRP) ~1.34 with return expectations added; note: ~3x higher turnover than ERC erodes returns significantly", "market_conditions": "Works: large heterogeneous multi-asset universes with unstable correlations; high macro uncertainty where traditional optimizers break down. Fails: small correlated universes (clustering adds no value); rapid correlation regime shifts (March 2020, 2022); high turnover punishes taxable accounts", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "VTI", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG", "EFA"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "high"}, {"name": "Credit Lead-Lag Signal (Excess Bond Premium Overlay)", "description": "Uses HYG/TLT spread as a proxy for the Gilchrist-Zakraj\u0161ek Excess Bond Premium (EBP) \u2014 the risk premium component of credit spreads stripped of default probability. A rapid widening in HYG-TLT spread (HYG falling relative to TLT) is a 3-18 month leading indicator of equity price declines. Used as an overlay: when 20-day rate-of-change in HYG/TLT turns sharply negative, reduce SPY/QQQ 25-50% and shift to TLT/GLD.", "evidence": ["Gilchrist & Zakraj\u0161ek (2012) 'Credit Spreads and Business Cycle Fluctuations' American Economic Review 102(4):1692-1720 \u2014 GZ spread substantially better than Baa-Aaa for predicting economic activity", "Favara, Gilchrist, Lewis & Zakraj\u0161ek (2016) 'Recession Risk and the Excess Bond Premium' Federal Reserve FEDS Notes \u2014 EBP orthogonal to current conditions predicts equity price declines", "Monthly EBP data freely available from Federal Reserve website, updated regularly"], "historical_sharpe": "Predictive overlay, not standalone strategy; GZ spread preceded 2001, 2008-09, and 2020 equity crashes; EBP innovations explain significant portion of subsequent equity returns over 3-18 month horizon", "market_conditions": "Works: credit-driven recessions and financial crises (2001, 2008-09). Fails: externally-driven shocks (COVID March 2020 \u2014 33% equity drop in 33 days with minimal lead time); Fed intervention immediately compresses spreads (March 23 2020 facility announcement); supply-side recessions", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "TLT", "GLD", "AGG"], "timeframe": "weekly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Defensive Asset Allocation (DAA \u2014 Keller Canary Universe)", "description": "Evolution of VAA that decouples the crash signal from asset selection. A 'canary' universe of just VWO+BND determines defensive posture (if either has negative momentum, go partially defensive). Momentum ranking handles offensive asset selection separately. Result: average cash/bond fraction drops from 50-60% (VAA) to 25-30%, improving CAGR while maintaining crash protection. Addresses VAA's excessive defensiveness in bull markets.", "evidence": ["Keller & Keuning (2018) 'Breadth Momentum and the Canary Universe: DAA' SSRN:3212862 \u2014 improves CAGR in 3 of 4 tested universes vs VAA", "Allocate Smartly independent verification \u2014 DAA reduces cash drag materially vs VAA while preserving most crash-protection properties", "Bold Asset Allocation (BAA, 2022) SSRN:4166845 \u2014 further refinement with aggressive/defensive universe separation"], "historical_sharpe": "~1.0\u20131.15 (slightly better CAGR than VAA with similar drawdown profile); specific Sharpe varies by universe configuration", "market_conditions": "Works: same as VAA but with better bull market participation due to lower cash drag. Fails: simultaneous crash of canary assets AND offensive assets (2022: VWO and BND both fell, triggering defensiveness, but TLT also fell); extreme fast reversals", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}], "recommendation": "Start with Cross-Asset Time-Series Momentum with Volatility Targeting (Strategy 2). It has the strongest academic grounding (Pitk\u00e4j\u00e4rvi et al. JFE 2020, AQR century-long evidence), the highest documented Sharpe improvement mechanism (+45% vs basic TSMOM from cross-asset signal diversification), and uses your full instrument set naturally. The three-layer structure (momentum rank \u2192 trend filter \u2192 vol-based sizing) is implementable with simple arithmetic on ETF closing prices at monthly rebalancing \u2014 no exotic data required. It also addresses the 2022 failure mode of pure risk parity (bonds falling with equities) by dynamically rotating out of TLT when its trend breaks. For a $1000 starting account, hold only the top 1-2 ranked ETFs above their 200-day SMA, sized by inverse 21-day vol; park the remainder in SHY or money market. If you want a simpler fallback with documented live track record, DAA (Strategy 8) requires just one monthly momentum calculation and is well-documented by Keller on SSRN with independent verification by Allocate Smartly."}
